name: Forest Black Pepper
methodDescription: Harvested by hand, sun-dried on traditional mats.
specimenCopy: The berries are picked green — they only turn black in the drying. The colour you've always known as black pepper isn't the colour it grew. It's the colour of what happened to it afterward.
offeringCopy: If you've read this far, you already know more about this pepper than its label could tell you.

sensoryProfile.aroma: Sharp wood smoke, crushed pine needle, citrus oil
sensoryProfile.flavor: Direct, searing heat with slow-blooming notes of resin and dried tea
sensoryProfile.appearance: Deep purple-black, heavily wrinkled, dense uniform berries
